history.text: A royal centre is thought to have existed at Oddernes before 800, and the church was built around 1040.

history.label: 16th century
history.text: Another important element in the development of Kristiansand was the harbor on the island of Flekkerøy, which was the most important on the Skagerrak beginning in the 16th century and was first fortified under.

In 1635, King Christian IV ordered his feudal seigneur, Palle Rosenkrantz, to move from Nedenes and build a royal palace on the island. Foundation to 1900 Christian IV (renowned for having founded many towns).
In 1666, Christianssand became a garrison town and was heavily fortified.
Christianssand experienced its first fire in 1734, which was devastating to the city.

history.label: 19th century
history.text: Another important development during the 19th century was the foundation in 1881 of Eg Sindssygeasyl, the second central psychiatric institution in Norway (after Gaustad).
The City of Kristiansand had a quarantine station for maritime traffic and hospital at Odderøy Island for cholera patients that opened in 1804.

It burned buildings as far as the cathedral, which had been rebuilt in brick after a previous fire in 1880. 1900 onward With the development of hydropower in southern Norway, the city gradually developed.
The most recent major fire, in 1892, left half the original section of the city in ashes.

history.label: 1980s
history.text: In the 1980s, industry and business in the city declined, in part because of the 1986 fire at the Hotel Caledonien.

history.label: Today
history.text: Grauthelleren (Grathelleren), located on Fidjane, is believed to be a Stone Age settlement.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Archaeological excavations to the east of Oddernes Church have uncovered rural settlements that existed during the centuries immediately before and after the start of the common era.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Together with a corresponding discovery in Rogaland, these settlements are unique in the Norwegian context; isolated farms, rather than villages, were the norm in ancient Norway.

history.label: Regional role
history.text: Before the stone church was built, one or perhaps two wooden post churches are believed to have stood on the same spot.
